what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that posts a short-lived, operational research-agent launch plan for block-by-block feedback and describes an anti-bias method for running multiple agents with identical grounded context but different role lenses.
observations: YAML frontmatter includes id, address, type block, source claude_code, state current, title, author_session, channel dragnet-development, a part_of link to board://item-2e030ebc, timestamps, and a filed history entry.
Markdown body opens with a top-level heading framing the research-agent plan as short-lived and feedback-seeking.
It distinguishes this operational plan from a durable client profile.
It states that agent outputs will also land in the same working space.
It names and explains 'THE ANTI-BIAS METHOD': every agent receives the same verbatim message, breakdown, and design map, and differs only by a LENS (role).
Agents are charged neutrally to read context, react, propose, critique, surface missing/risky/valuable items, and explicitly not converge with the brief or each other.
It asserts that diversity comes from who the agents are, not from the author's framing.
No code symbols are present in the file.
standouts: The explicit anti-bias method: identical grounded context plus differing role lens.
The non-convergence rule directed at the agents themselves.
Its self-described short-lived operational scope and link to a parent noticeboard item.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and Markdown body.
Uses a top-level Markdown heading and explanatory paragraphs.
Uses an ALL CAPS section header to name a method.
Uses em dashes and parenthetical asides for qualification.
Contains no code blocks or symbol definitions.
concerns: The anti-bias claim cannot be verified from this file alone because the actual grounded context and lenses are not included.
The 'short-lived' status is asserted but no expiry condition or lifecycle rule is visible.
The non-convergence instruction is procedural rather than enforced by any visible mechanism.
notes: A noticeboard block item that proposes a multi-agent research plan and defines an anti-bias method using shared context plus role lenses.
questions: What exactly is the 'grounded context' (verbatim message, breakdown, design map) referenced in the method?
How are role lenses selected, assigned, and documented for each agent run?
What is the parent item board://item-2e030ebc and how does this plan relate to it?
How will agent outputs be ingested back into this working space?
What criteria determine when this short-lived item is retired?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a feedback-seeking, short-lived agent-launch plan that also documents an anti-bias execution method.
apparent_themes: Anti-bias through symmetric context and asymmetric role lenses
intention_signals: It is trying to solicit structured feedback on a research-agent launch plan while establishing a procedural guard against prompt-induced consensus by giving each agent the same evidence but a different interpretive role.
novelty: The explicit 'anti-bias method' that separates grounded context from role lens and forbids agents from converging with the brief or each other is a distinct procedural design.
contribution: It contributes a named operational method for running diverse research agents (shared context, role lens, non-convergence rule) and a short-lived planning artifact tied to a parent noticeboard item.
summary_for_embedding: This noticeboard block item is a short-lived operational research-agent launch plan that defines an anti-bias method where every agent receives identical grounded context but a different role lens and is forbidden from converging with the brief or each other.
intention_for_embedding: It was filed to invite block-by-block feedback on launching research agents and to document the anti-bias procedure for their execution.
